feedback: The onboarding handoff broke twice and we had to repeat the same setup steps with three different agents. This now feels like a repeated operational risk.
Billing still shows duplicate charges and finance has not resolved the credits after multiple follow-ups. Leads have started tracking it as a high-priority issue.
Support acknowledged the outage quickly but the root cause explanation stayed vague and incomplete. It is affecting trust in the rollout plan.
The documentation points to screens that no longer exist, which slowed the team down during rollout. The team flagged it as needing executive attention.
We lost confidence in the platform after another reliability incident during a busy reporting window. It is creating avoidable friction in the weekly review cycle.
Response times from support are too inconsistent for an operations workflow that depends on same-day answers. This now feels like a repeated operational risk.
The latest release fixed one bug but introduced two new issues in the approval workflow. Leads have started tracking it as a high-priority issue.
Pricing is difficult to justify when the reporting exports fail this often. It is affecting trust in the rollout plan.
The dashboard keeps timing out when the team tries to review week-over-week performance. The team flagged it as needing executive attention.
Training materials were generic and did not prepare the team for the actual operational edge cases we hit. It is creating avoidable friction in the weekly review cycle.
Another invoice discrepancy forced the operations team to pause work and escalate billing manually. This now feels like a repeated operational risk.
The incident communication was delayed, and the status updates did not explain expected recovery time clearly. Leads have started tracking it as a high-priority issue.

The product covered the core workflow, but the team still needed workarounds for reporting and billing. The team can work with it for now.
Support eventually resolved the ticket, although the path to resolution was slower than expected. There is still room to tighten execution.
The onboarding sessions were organized, but the materials still need clearer examples for administrators. It is acceptable but not yet strong.
Documentation answered the basic questions, yet it did not cover the more advanced operational scenarios. The experience is stable enough for light usage.
The reporting experience was usable for light reviews, but larger exports remained mixed. The team can work with it for now.
Pricing conversations are still open, and the value feels dependent on future reliability improvements. There is still room to tighten execution.

The support manager stepped in quickly and unblocked a critical issue before the end of the day. That improvement stood out clearly in this cycle.
Once the workflow was configured correctly, the team saw faster routing and fewer manual handoffs. The team wants that pattern repeated elsewhere.
The latest onboarding checkpoint was clearer than before and reduced confusion for new administrators. It helped offset some of the broader frustration.
Documentation updates around permissions were helpful and removed one of the recurring setup blockers. That improvement stood out clearly in this cycle.
The team appreciated the proactive outreach after the last incident and the follow-up was more transparent. The team wants that pattern repeated elsewhere.
